content: Just as intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) drive AI reinforcement learning agents to explore sparse-reward environments by rewarding the agent for encountering unpredictable states, biological agents (from the biological definition of 'agent') evolved intrinsic curiosity as an implicit reward signal to drive exploration of unoccupied niches even when immediate survival rewards are sparse. This shared principle suggests that all goal-directed agents, whether artificial reinforcement learning agents or biological living agents, rely on intrinsic curiosity to overcome the limitation of sparse extrinsic rewards.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
